fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the work center.

fields.name: Search Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternate name that you can use to search for the record in question when you cannot remember the value in the Name field.

fields.name: Name 2
fields.tooltip:
Specifies an additional part of the work center name.
The Name 2 field can be used, for example, if the work center has a long name.
You can enter a maximum of 30 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Address
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the address of the work center.
You can enter a maximum of 30 characters, both numbers and letters.
The contents of the Address field are often used when you print something, so you should enter the address as you want it to appear.

fields.name: Address 2
fields.tooltip:
Specifies an additional line with the work center address.
You can enter a maximum of 30 characters, both numbers and letters.
The contents of the Address 2 field are often used when you print something, so you should enter the address as you want it to appear.

fields.name: City
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the city of the work center address.
How the Post Code and City Fields Are Filled In

fields.name: Post Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the work center's post code.
How the Post Code and City Fields Are Filled In

fields.name: Alternate Work Center
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternate work center.

fields.name: Work Center Group Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the work center group, if the work center or underlying machine center is assigned to a work center group.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the global dimension that is linked to the record or entry for analysis purposes. Two global dimensions, typically for the company's most important activities, are available on all cards, documents, reports, and lists.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the global dimension that is linked to the record or entry for analysis purposes. Two global dimensions, typically for the company's most important activities, are available on all cards, documents, reports, and lists.

fields.name: Subcontractor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a subcontractor who supplies this work center.

fields.name: Direct Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the selected item or resource.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the center's cost that includes indirect costs, such as machine maintenance.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Queue Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the queue time of the work center.

fields.name: Queue Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the queue time unit of measure code.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the work center card was last modified.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether a comment has been entered for this work center.
To view a comment or create a new one, click Related Information, point to Work Ctr., and then click Comments. A window appears that displays existing comments and allow you to create new ones.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox. Notes follow documents when they are posted in the same way as comments.
The program automatically returns the entry to the Comment field if there is an entry in the Comments window.
You cannot edit or delete the contents of this field.
See also the Manufacturing Comment Line table.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Capacity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the capacity of the work center.

fields.name: Efficiency
fields.tooltip: Specifies the efficiency factor as a percentage of the work center.

fields.name: Maximum Efficiency
fields.tooltip: Specifies the maximum efficiency factor of the work center.

fields.name: Minimum Efficiency
fields.tooltip: Specifies the minimum efficiency factor of the work center.

fields.name: Calendar Rounding Precision
fields.tooltip: Specifies how calendar entries are rounded, such as whether minutes are rounded to hours.

fields.name: Simulation Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the simulation type for the work center.

fields.name: Shop Calendar Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the shop calendar code that the planning of this work center refers to.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the work center is blocked from being posted in transactions, for example, if the machine center is out of order.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If the field contains a starting and ending date for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval. For example, the ledger entries for the work center within this interval are displayed in the Capacity Ledger Entry table. The values in the quantity fields are shown for the interval only.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Work Shift Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a work shift filter.
If the field contains a work shift code, the values in the quantities fields are exclusively based on entries to the work shift code that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are specific rules for how these can be combined:

Meaning
Sample Expression
Included

Equal to

1

Entries from work shift 1 (the work shift code can contain letters instead of just numbers)


Interval

1..5

Entries from work shifts 1 through 5


Either/or

1|2

Entries that are from either work shift 1 or work shift 2


Different from

<>1

Entries from all work shifts except work shift 1


To see the work shift codes in the Work Shift table, click the field.

fields.name: Capacity (Total)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total available capacity of the work center.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Capacity (Total) field in the Calendar Entry table.
You can filter this field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of specific dates and work shifts. The unit of measure for the Capacity (Total) field is set in the Unit of Measure Code field.
To see the calendar entries that make up the number shown, click the field.
The program calculates the calendar entries of a work center when you run the Calculate Work Center Calendar batch job.

fields.name: Capacity (Effective)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the effective available capacity of the work center.

fields.name: Prod. Order Need (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the calculated capacity requirements for production orders at this work center.

fields.name: Prod. Order Need Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected costs, which this work center requires for production of the calculated production orders.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field by using the Expected Operation Cost Amt. field in the Prod. Order Routing Line table.
You can filter this field so that its contents are only calculated on the basis of the status of production orders.

fields.name: Prod. Order Status Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a production order status filter.
If the field contains a production order status, the values in the fields that contain quantities are only based on entries with the production order status that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are specific rules for how these can be combined:


Meaning


Sample Expression


Included


Equal to

Planned

Entries from planned production orders.


Interval

Planned..In Process

Entries from production orders within the status Planned through In Process.


Either/or

Planned|Firm Planned

Entries that are from either production orders with the status Planned or Firm Planned.


Different from

<>Simulated

Entries from all production orders except production orders with the status Simulated.


To see the production order status filters that are available, click the field.

fields.name: Unit Cost Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit cost calculation that is to be made.

fields.name: Specific Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies where to define the unit costs. If you place a check mark in this field, you can define the unit costs on the routing line. This allows you to have individual costs on every routing line. This is useful for subcontracting operations with varying rates.

fields.name: Consolidated Calendar
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the consolidated calendar is used.

fields.name: Flushing Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method to use to calculate and handle output at the work center. Manual: Output must be posted manually by using the output journal. Forward: Output is automatically calculated and posted when you change the status of a simulated, planned (or firm planned) production order to Released. You can still post output manually from the output journal. Backward: Output is automatically calculated and posted when you change the status of a released production order to Finished or when the last operation is finished. You can still post output manually from the output journal. The setting you make in this field is copied to the Flushing Method field on the production order routing line according to the machine/work center of the master routing, but you can change the field for an individual production order to allow a different output (or consumption) posting of that order.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the number series that was used to assign the work center number.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip: Specifies the overhead rate of this work center.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the county of the subcontractor that the work center represents.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region code of the work center.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location where the work center operates by default.

fields.name: Open Shop Floor Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin that functions as the default open shop floor bin at the work center.

fields.name: To-Production Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin in the production area where components that are picked for production are placed by default before they can be consumed.

fields.name: From-Production Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin in the production area where finished end items are taken by default when the process involves warehouse activity.
